stem: A fitness researcher collected data on the number of hours of vigorous exercise per week ($x$) and the resting heart rate in beats per minute ($y$) for a group of adults. The line of best fit for the data is $y = -2.3x + 84$. One participant exercises 6 hours per week and has an actual resting heart rate of 68 beats per minute. What is the residual for this participant?
explanation:
The residual is defined as: residual = actual value $-$ predicted value.

Step 1: Find the predicted resting heart rate for a participant who exercises 6 hours per week using the line of best fit.
$$y = -2.3(6) + 84 = -13.8 + 84 = 70.2 \text{ bpm}$$

Step 2: Calculate the residual.
$$\text{residual} = \text{actual} - \text{predicted} = 68 - 70.2 = -2.2$$

The residual is $-2.2$, meaning the participant's actual resting heart rate is 2.2 bpm lower than what the line of best fit predicts.

distractorRationale.A: Correct. The residual is actual minus predicted: $68 - 70.2 = -2.2$.
distractorRationale.B: Sign error. This results from computing predicted minus actual ($70.2 - 68 = 2.2$) instead of actual minus predicted.
distractorRationale.C: Incomplete solution. This is the participant's actual resting heart rate — the problem's given value — not the residual.
distractorRationale.D: Incomplete solution. This is the predicted resting heart rate from the line of best fit ($-2.3(6) + 84 = 70.2$), but the residual requires subtracting the predicted value from the actual value.
